segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of a hand performing a card trick, sliding a red-backed playing card into a smartphone's screen, which displays a patterned wallpaper. The scene transitions to a man in a white shirt seated at a green table, holding a smartphone and a clear plastic bottle with a red liquid inside. He pours the liquid into a glass, then places the phone into the bottle, which is then sealed and shaken. The phone is removed, now seemingly unharmed, and the man displays its screen, which is fully functional. The scene shifts to a vintage television frame showing a man in a black suit performing a card trick for a group of well-dressed men, including one labeled 'JEFF BEZOS' with a red arrow pointing to him. The video then cuts to a young man in a gray Levi's t-shirt sitting at a desk with a microphone and a monitor displaying 'CARD MAGIC PRO' in white text with a clover logo. He speaks directly to the camera, gesturing with his hands, in a well-lit room with acoustic panels and a desk lamp in the background.

segments.caption: The video opens with the Young Man in Gray Shirt seated at a desk, speaking directly to the camera. He is wearing a gray Levi's t-shirt and is positioned in front of a black Monitor displaying 'CARD MAGIC PRO' with a clover logo. A black Microphone on a stand is in front of him, and behind him, a modern white Desk Lamp and black-and-white Acoustic Panels are visible, indicating a sound-treated room. He gestures with his right hand as he speaks, then the scene transitions to a close-up of hands performing a card trick on a green felt surface. A black Smartphone is held in one hand, and a playing card is placed on its screen. The card is then removed, revealing it to be the Two of Clubs. The hands then interact with the phone, tapping the screen to show an image of the same card, suggesting a digital or augmented reality element. The card is placed back on the phone, and the screen displays a photo of the card, reinforcing the trick. Text appears on screen: 'LINK IN DESCRIPTION LEARN A CARD FORCE', indicating an instructional or promotional element. The hands continue to manipulate the card and phone, emphasizing the magic trick’s mechanics.

segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of a pair of hands holding a black smartphone over a green felt surface. A small stack of red-backed playing cards is placed on the phone's screen, which is initially dark. The hands manipulate the cards, sliding them across the screen and then lifting one to reveal it as the Two of Clubs. The scene transitions to a black screen with the number '2.' displayed, indicating a segment or step. The next sequence shows a man in a white button-up shirt seated at the green felt table, holding the same smartphone, now displaying a colorful iOS home screen with various app icons. He rotates the phone, showcasing its screen, then uses his finger to swipe through the home screen, revealing a dock with frequently used apps. The lighting is focused on the hands and phone, with a dark background emphasizing the action. The man’s movements are deliberate and precise, suggesting a demonstration or tutorial. The green felt surface remains consistent throughout, providing a clean, contrasting backdrop for the smartphone and hands.

segments.caption: The video opens with the Man in White Shirt holding a Smartphone with a colorful iOS home screen, rotating it to display the interface while seated at the Green Felt Surface. He then transitions to a magic trick, placing the phone screen-down and revealing a red fabric that appears to emerge from the device. The scene shifts to a close-up of his hands manipulating a transparent plastic sheet with a cut-out oval shape, which is then placed over the phone’s screen. An animated overlay appears, illustrating the plastic sheet’s placement and alignment with the phone’s notch. The man demonstrates how the sheet creates an illusion by covering the screen, and then uses a red fabric to simulate pulling something from the phone, reinforcing the trick. The video concludes with the man placing the plastic sheet over the phone and using the red fabric to complete the illusion, all set against the consistent green felt background.

segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of hands on a green felt surface, peeling a transparent plastic sheet from a black smartphone. The hands then use a red fabric to wipe the phone, revealing a cracked screen. The phone is rotated, and its screen lights up to show the iOS home screen with the time 12:50. The hands then place a transparent plastic sheet with a cut-out over the phone, aligning it with the notch, and use the red fabric to simulate pulling an object from the screen, creating a visual illusion. The scene transitions to a young man in a gray Levi's t-shirt seated at a desk with a microphone and a monitor displaying 'CARD MAGIC PRO'. He speaks directly to the camera, gesturing with his hands. The video cuts to a book cover titled 'MIND-BLOWING MAGIC TRICKS FOR EVERYONE' by Oscar Owen, featuring a black-and-white image of hands fanning playing cards. The book is shown open, displaying step-by-step instructions for a card trick with images and text. The video then returns to the young man, who continues speaking, before cutting to a close-up of hands cutting an orange on a plate, revealing a playing card inside. The final shot shows the book cover again, this time with the text '50+ INCREDIBLE TRICKS INSIDE' appearing on the right side of the screen.

segments.caption: The video begins with the Young Man in Gray Shirt seated at a desk in a well-lit room with acoustic panels and a desk lamp, speaking directly to the camera. He is positioned in front of a Monitor displaying 'CARD MAGIC PRO' with a clover logo, and a Microphone is placed in front of him. He gestures with his hands as he speaks, appearing to introduce or explain something. The scene then transitions to a different setting: the Man in White Shirt is now seated at a Green Felt Surface, performing a card trick. He places a Smartphone on the table, then begins shuffling a deck of red-backed Playing Cards. He splits the deck into two piles, places the phone on one pile, and then spreads the cards out in a fan to reveal their faces. He picks up the phone, which now displays a red-backed card, and places it on top of a physical card, revealing the Queen of Diamonds. The entire sequence is performed with precise hand movements, emphasizing the magic trick’s mechanics and the interaction between the physical cards and the smartphone.

segments.caption: The video opens with the Man in White Shirt seated at a Green Felt Surface, holding a Smartphone in one hand and a Playing Card (Queen of Diamonds) in the other. He places the card on the table and uses the phone’s camera to capture its image, then flips the card to reveal the Ten of Spades, which he also photographs. He then navigates the phone’s settings, selects a photo of a red-backed card as the lock screen wallpaper, and sets it. After placing the phone face-down on the table, he shuffles a deck of red-backed cards, then places the phone on top of the deck. He picks up the phone, revealing the lock screen with the red-backed card image, and then places it back down. The sequence ends with him picking up the phone again, suggesting a magic trick involving the phone and cards.

segments.caption: The video opens with the Man in White Shirt seated at a Green Felt Surface, performing a card trick. He places a Smartphone on a small stack of red-backed Playing Cards, then picks up the phone and uses it to photograph a card, the Ten of Spades, which he then sets as the lock screen wallpaper. He proceeds to flip through the cards, revealing the Queen of Diamonds, and then places the phone back on the deck. The scene transitions to a black screen with the number '5.' displayed, followed by the man holding the Smartphone in one hand and revealing a collection of miniature app icons in his other palm, seemingly pulled from the phone’s screen. He gestures with the phone, swiping through the home screen, while the tiny icons remain in his hand, creating a visual illusion of apps being physically extracted. The lighting is focused on his hands and the phone, with a dark background emphasizing the trick’s details. The sequence ends with him holding the phone and the icons, maintaining the illusion.

segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of the Man in White Shirt holding a Smartphone, rotating it to display its colorful iOS home screen. He swipes through the screen, then performs a magic trick by making miniature App Icons appear in his palm as if they've been extracted from the phone. The scene transitions to a black screen with white text reading '6. (My Favourite Trick!)', followed by a Vintage Television Frame showing a group of men, including one labeled 'JEFF BEZOS', watching a card trick. The video then cuts back to the Man in White Shirt seated at a Green Felt Surface, shuffling a deck of red-backed Playing Cards. He places three cards face-down on the table, picks up the Smartphone, and opens the calculator app, tapping the screen as if performing a calculation related to the trick. The lighting is focused on his hands and the table, creating a dramatic, intimate atmosphere for the magic demonstration.

segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of the Man in White Shirt seated at a Green Felt Surface, holding a Smartphone displaying a calculator app. He rapidly inputs numbers, starting with 453, then adding 314 to reach 767, followed by multiplying by 1000 to arrive at 767,000, and finally adding 410 to display 768,410. To the right of the phone, three face-down Playing Cards are laid out. He then flips them over one by one, revealing the 7 of Clubs, 6 of Hearts, 8 of Spades, 4 of Clubs, and 10 of Diamonds — the digits of the number 768,410. He gathers the cards, shuffles them briefly, and places them face down. The camera angle shifts slightly as he resets the calculator to 0, then begins a new calculation: entering 405, adding 100, and multiplying by 4 to get 435,810. The cards remain on the table, and a deck of red-backed cards is visible to the right. The lighting is focused on the hands and objects, with a dark background emphasizing the green felt surface. The entire sequence suggests a mathematical card trick where the numbers on the cards correspond to a calculated result.

segments.caption: The video segment opens with a close-up of a hand holding a black smartphone displaying a calculator app with a '0' on the screen, resting on a green felt surface. The hand places the phone down and picks up a deck of red-backed playing cards, fanning them to reveal the 7 of Clubs, 6 of Hearts, 8 of Spades, 4 of Clubs, and 10 of Diamonds. The cards are then squared and shuffled with precise, practiced movements, including a riffle shuffle and a cut, demonstrating dexterity. The hand continues to manipulate the deck, fanning and squaring the cards repeatedly, emphasizing control and fluidity. The smartphone remains visible in the lower right corner, screen off, throughout the sequence. The green felt surface provides a consistent, textured backdrop, and the lighting remains steady and focused on the hands and cards, highlighting the intricate movements and the red design of the card backs. No other objects or people are visible in the frame, keeping the focus entirely on the card handling and the smartphone.

segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of the Man in White Shirt’s hands manipulating a deck of red-backed Playing Cards on a Green Felt Surface. He shuffles and fans the cards, revealing a sequence of face cards including the 7 of Clubs, 6 of Hearts, 8 of Spades, 4 of Clubs, and 10 of Diamonds. He then places the cards face-down and picks up the Smartphone, which displays a calculator app. He inputs a series of numbers, culminating in the display of '435,810'. He places the phone down and arranges three cards face-up on the table. The scene transitions to a black screen with the number '7.' in white text. The next scene shows the Man in White Shirt holding the Smartphone and a clear Plastic Bottle. He places the phone into the bottle, which is then sealed with a cap. The phone’s screen remains on, showing the time '09:55' and date 'Saturday 21 November'. He shakes the bottle gently, and the phone’s screen updates to '09:56', indicating the phone is still functional despite being submerged in the bottle. The lighting is focused on the hands and objects, with a dark background emphasizing the actions on the green felt surface.

segments.caption: The video opens with the Man in White Shirt seated at a green felt table, holding a Smartphone in one hand and a clear Plastic Bottle in the other. He places the phone down, then picks it up again, revealing its screen displaying the time '09:55' and the date 'Saturday 21 November'. He then carefully inserts the smartphone into the empty plastic bottle, ensuring the screen remains visible through the transparent plastic. He rotates the bottle, demonstrating that the phone is fully enclosed yet still functional, with the screen lighting up and displaying the time. After a few seconds, he removes the phone, which appears undamaged, and places it back on the table. The scene transitions to a black screen with a white number '8' in the center, followed by a new setup: the same man now holds the smartphone, which displays a calculator app, and a small clear Glass filled with water. He tilts the phone over the glass, and a single drop of water falls from the phone’s screen into the glass, creating a visual illusion that the phone is leaking water. The lighting remains consistent and focused, with a dark background emphasizing the actions on the green felt surface.

segments.caption: The video segment begins with the Man in White Shirt seated at a green felt table, holding a black Smartphone in one hand and a clear Glass filled with water in the other. He places the phone screen-down over the glass, and a single drop of water appears to fall from the phone’s screen into the glass, creating a visual illusion. The scene shifts to a close-up of the table, where the phone is placed screen-up, displaying a blue abstract wallpaper, and the man’s hand gestures toward it. He then picks up the phone, flips it to show the back, and proceeds to demonstrate a trick by placing the phone into a clear Glass (now empty) and then removing it, revealing the glass is still empty. He repeats the action with a second glass, maintaining the illusion. The scene transitions to a black screen with white text reading '9. (BONUS TRICK)'. The man reappears, now holding a gold-colored Smartphone (different from the previous black one), and performs a new trick by placing a small, glowing object (possibly a light or LED) near the phone, which then appears to emit light from the screen. He places the phone on the table, and the screen displays a control center interface with various app icons, suggesting a digital interaction or illusion. The lighting is dim, focusing attention on the phone and the man’s hands, emphasizing the magical effect.

segments.caption: The video begins with a close-up of a person’s hands on a green felt surface, interacting with a black smartphone displaying a control center interface. The person’s right index finger is wrapped in a beige finger sleeve, which they adjust and then use to tap the phone screen. The screen briefly turns off, then back on, showing a battery icon. The person then uses the finger sleeve to tap the screen again, causing a glowing red light to appear on the tip of the finger sleeve, suggesting a digital or light-based illusion. The scene transitions to a wider shot of the Man in White Shirt, seated at the green felt table in a dimly lit environment, holding a gold-colored smartphone in one hand and the finger sleeve in the other. He performs a similar tapping motion, with the finger sleeve glowing red as if reacting to the phone. The video ends with a black screen displaying white text: 'PLEASE SUBSCRIBE CHECK OUT THE BOOK BELOW', indicating a call to action for viewers.
